---
title: "Aim4 _Ver_1"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-20"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Load Library
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
```


This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r Load datasets}
# Load in the filtered and wrangled Parkinson's metadata
parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat = read.csv("../../Datasets/Metadata/parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat.csv", row.names = 1)

# Load in feature table exported from QIIME2
counts = read.delim('../../QIIME2_outputs/feature-table.txt', skip = 1, row.names = 1)

# Load in taxonomy table exported from QIIME2
taxonomy = read.delim('../../QIIME2_outputs/taxonomy.tsv', row.names = 1)

# Counts
counts_formatted = counts %>% as.matrix()
# Create the phyloseq object
# Taxonomy
taxonomy_formatted = taxonomy %>% 
  separate(col = Taxon, 
           into = c("Domain","Phylum","Class","Order",
                    "Family","Genus","Species"),
           sep = ";", fill = "right") %>% 
  select(-Confidence) %>% 
  as.matrix()

# Counts
counts_formatted = counts %>% as.matrix()
ps = phyloseq(sample_data(parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat),
              otu_table(counts_formatted, taxa_are_rows = T),
              tax_table(taxonomy_formatted))

```

```{r Load the indicator taxa}
#Extract the indicator taxa obtained from aim2 
taxa_ids <- c(
  #"630532cabb74720c1204579e6fe741b4",  # Cloacibacillus
  #"a030e5d5ff9c75a027e6cc58ff02cf7c" # Alloprevotella
  "4c8288bfbd76958c0c094d87b97650f8" #Escherichia-Shigella
)
ps_relab <- transform_sample_counts(ps, function(x) x / sum(x))
#Extract the phyloseq abundance
otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_relab))

# make samples = rows
if (taxa_are_rows(ps_relab)) {
  otu <- t(otu)
}

# keep only your  taxa
sub <- otu[, taxa_ids, drop = FALSE]
```


```{r Create Patient Scoring}
score_pa <- rowSums(sub > 0.01)
score_abundance <- rowSums(sub)

z <- scale(sub)
score_z <- rowSums(z, na.rm = TRUE)

```


```{r Add to Metadata}
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_relab))

# Give the first column a simple name, e.g., "Sub1"
# Take the first column of `sub` and give it a simple name
meta$Sub1 <- sub[,1]


meta$score_pa <- score_pa[rownames(meta)]
meta$score_abundance <- score_abundance[rownames(meta)]
meta$score_z <- score_z[rownames(meta)]
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r Predict the MoCA based on the indicator taxa}
model2 <- lm(moca_total ~ Sub1 + Age + Sex, data = meta)
summary(model2)
```
Age and E.shigella (0.0230) seems to be the  predictors for MoCA score. 
```{r Generate Plot for Aim4}

library(ggplot2)

# Create a sequence of Sub1 values
sub_seq <- seq(min(meta$Sub1, na.rm = TRUE),
               max(meta$Sub1, na.rm = TRUE),
               length.out = 100)

# Create newdata holding Age at mean and Sex as reference
newdata <- data.frame(
  Sub1 = sub_seq,
  Age = mean(meta$Age, na.rm = TRUE),
  Sex = "Female"  # adjust to your reference group
)

# Predict MoCA with confidence intervals
pred <- predict(model2, newdata, interval = "confidence")
plot_df <- cbind(newdata, pred)

# Plot
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Sub1, y = fit)) +
  geom_line(size = 1, color = "blue") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_point(data = meta, aes(x = Sub1, y = moca_total), alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(x = "Indicator Taxa Score (Presence of Escherichia-Shigella in PD patients) ",
       y = "Predicted MoCA Score",
       title = "Adjusted regression of MoCA on indicator taxa Escherichia-Shigella") + 
  theme_classic() +
 theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12)   # change number to what you like
  )

```
